items.title: Prices for new U.S. cars have soared. Here’s 1 big reason why
items.summary_auto: The affordability problem is a risk to American carmakers as Chinese brands expand globally. Many lower and middle-income car shoppers have been relegated to the used-car lot.

items.title: 2nd round of polygraphs conducted in disappearance of N.S. children
items.summary_auto: Nova Scotia RCMP have conducted a second round of polygraph examinations in the disappearance of Jack and Lilly Sullivan, with two of those tests focused on one of the only pieces of physical evidence in the case: a light pink blanket found hanging in a tree.

items.title: N.L. judge criticizes 'inflammatory rhetoric' on bail reform, as he grants bail to repeat offender
items.summary_auto: A Newfoundland judge was critical of the "jail not bail" movement during a hearing on Tuesday in Harbour Grace, where a man accused of repeatedly stealing from homes in the Conception Bay North area was granted bail.

items.title: Horses 'still dying' after flights from Canada to Japan — and regulator seems unaware
items.summary_auto: A new investigation by animal rights advocates shows horses being flown for slaughter in Japan continue to suffer injuries, illness and even death, reigniting the call for Canada to ban the shipments.

items.title: Calls grow for Paralympics to be moved earlier as slushy conditions cause chaos
items.summary_auto: There are a few common refrains repeated in most of the news articles and athlete announcements about the 2026 Milano-Cortino Paralympic Games: challenging, extremely difficult and brutal conditions.

items.title: First Nation in northern Ontario praises mining company for 'true reconciliation'
items.summary_auto: After hearing concerns from Biigtigong Nishnaabeg in northern Ontario about potential exploration in an area known as the Seeley property, Landore Resources Canada Inc. agreed to withdraw its application to renew its mining lease there. Here's why the First Nation is praising the company for “demonstrating true reconciliation,” and what lessons it says others can learn from this approach.

items.title: Conservation groups worry Carney's new nature strategy won't come with funding
items.summary_auto: The Carney government's strategy to protect nature is expected to be released in the coming weeks — and some nature advocacy groups worry it won't come with any new funding.

items.title: Mobile brain scans sound like science fiction. But they're now helping more Canadian patients
items.summary_auto: A rising number of Canadian facilities are using mobile brain scanners to help patients, by offering MRIs mid-surgery in hospital or diagnosing stroke right on the roadside. So what's the future of this game-changing technology?

items.title: Start screening for colorectal cancers earlier, Canadian Cancer Society urges
items.summary_auto: People as young as 45 should be invited to provincial and territorial programs to screen for colorectal cancer, says the Canadian Cancer Society. The call comes as emerging evidence suggests younger adults are increasingly being diagnosed with the disease before they may be eligible for screening.

items.title: NDP MP Lori Idlout crossing floor to Liberals, bringing Carney closer to majority
Black rain is falling in Iran after strikes on oil facilities. Scientists warn of long-term consequences
items.summary_auto: The World Health Organization issued a warning Tuesday about toxic pollutants in the air after U.S.-Israeli strikes on oil depots in and around Tehran raised thick, dark clouds that produced black, oily rain. Scientists tell CBC News the strikes have raised serious health and environmental concerns that could impact water and food sources long after the smoke and black rain clears.

items.title: Hawaii's Kilauea volcano sends fiery lava 400 metres into the air
items.summary_auto: The latest lava fountaining episode of an erupting Hawaii volcano shot 400 metres high Tuesday, prompting temporary closures at a national park and part of an important highway because of falling volcanic fragments, including ash.

items.title: Tens of thousands of dollars in damage after Pokemon cards stolen in B.C.'s Lower Mainland
items.summary_auto: Pokemon cards are becoming so valuable that B.C. card shops are becoming vulnerable to break and enters, according to the owner of an Abbotsford store that was recently broken into.

items.title: Woman charged with attempted murder after weekend shooting at Rihanna's L.A. home
items.summary_auto: A Florida woman accused of firing gunshots at Rihanna's home in Los Angeles was charged Tuesday with one count of attempted murder and other felony offences, including 10 counts of assault on a person with a semi-automatic firearm.

items.title: Freezing rain storm headed for Ont., Que., Atlantic provinces | Hanomansing Tonight
items.summary_auto: A major storm is expected to sweep through parts of Eastern Canada, potentially causing power outages and dangerous road conditions.

items.title: How high will the U.S.-Israel war with Iran push gas prices? | About That
items.summary_auto: Oil and gas prices are rapidly rising around the world amid the U.S.-Israel war with Iran. Andrew Chang explains what's driving the surge and why predicting the next moves in the oil market is so difficult. Images provided by The Canadian Press, Reuters and Getty Images

items.title: Mexico insists it’s safe for the World Cup — others see the shadows
items.summary_auto: As Mexican officials project an image of safety and stability ahead of the FIFA World Cup this summer, others are urging visitors to take notice of the darker forces that make the country unsafe for its own citizens.

items.title: Australia extends asylum to 2 more Iranian women's soccer team members
items.summary_auto: Two more members of the Iranian women's soccer team were granted asylum in Australia before their teammates departed, the country's Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke said Wednesday.

items.title: Early detection prevented worse outcomes in Calgary E. coli outbreak at 11 daycares: study
items.summary_auto: A standardized public health response to an E. coli outbreak at several Calgary daycare centres three years ago resulted in fewer children developing serious health complications, a new study suggests.

items.title: Early detection after E. coli outbreak at Calgary daycares prevented worse outcomes: study
items.summary_auto: A standardized public health response to an E. coli outbreak at several Calgary daycare centres three years ago resulted in fewer children developing serious health complications, a new study suggests.

items.title: Paralympic viewing guide: Mark Arendz eyes 14th career medal
items.summary_auto: CBC Sports' daily newsletter recaps Day 4 of the Paralympic Winter Games and looks at the top Canadians to watch on Wednesday.

items.title: Former CBC News host Travis Dhanraj says he was 'pulled off the air' for fighting against bias
items.summary_auto: Former CBC News host Travis Dhanraj accused the public broadcaster of trying to silence him when he challenged bias at the network and fought for diversity of opinion.

items.title: Regina man imprisoned for crystal meth-fuelled crime spree that ended in fatal collision
items.summary_auto: Mihail Oblacov's life hit "rock bottom essentially right out of the gate" after he was introduced to crystal meth, his lawyer says. His actions while he was in the throes of his addiction in 2024 caused the death of a stranger.

items.title: Up to 16 Liberals considering support for NDP bill to stop Canadian arms flowing to other countries from U.S.
Up to 16 Liberals considering support for NDP's arms-control legislation
items.summary_auto: When the NDP's arms-control bill that seeks to end largely permit-free Canadian military exports to the U.S. is put to a vote on Wednesday, it could garner the support of more than a dozen Liberal MPs. That would be the first time some in caucus split from the government's position on legislation under Prime Minister Mark Carney.

items.title: Canada raises alarm about escalating violence in Lebanon
items.summary_auto: The Canadian government said Tuesday it is alarmed by the escalation of violence and attacks in Lebanon, as the war in the Middle East expands. It called on all sides to uphold humanitarian law.

items.title: Vancouver Coun. Sean Orr sues Mayor Ken Sim for defamation
items.summary_auto: Vancouver Coun. Sean Orr is suing Mayor Ken Sim for allegedly defamatory comments Sim made at an event for members of Chinese-language media in February.

items.title: IN PHOTOS | First Nations leaders unveil items repatriated from Vatican
items.summary_auto: First Nations leaders unveiled five items repatriated to their communities after being at the Vatican for more than a century, during a ceremony at the Canadian Museum of History in Gatineau, Que., on Tuesday.

items.title: Ottawa spending $229M to help tariff-hit Ontario workers obtain new skills
items.summary_auto: The federal government will spend $228.8 million over the next three years to help Ontario workers in industries hit hard by U.S. tariffs acquire new skills and adapt to the trade war disruption.

items.title: Heated Rivalry stars Hudson Williams and Francois Arnaud denounce hateful comments
items.summary_auto: Actors Hudson Williams and Francois Arnaud posted matching statements to their Instagram accounts on Monday night, calling out hateful comments made by fans of the hit hockey romance show.

items.title: WNBA, players' union to meet as deadline nears to start season on time: report
items.summary_auto: The WNBA and players union met for nearly 12 hours trying to get a new collective bargaining agreement deal done to start the upcoming season on time.
